heroHeadline: UN-EU-WB: Gaza Reconstruction Needs $71.4B; IDF Strikes Hamas Operatives as BoP Talks Continue
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. A joint UN-EU-World Bank assessment published Apr 21 found Gaza needs $71.4 billion over 10 years for recovery — a 77-year human development setback — dwarfing the less than $1B of $17B pledged reconstruction funds received. The IDF eliminated two Hamas operatives in central Gaza on Apr 21. Trump's Board of Peace held talks with UAE's DP World on Gaza supply chain logistics. Hamas continues to reject the eight-month disarmament framework; Cairo backchannel contacts ongoing after two missed deadlines (Apr 14 and Apr 19). Hamas proposed handing over only police/internal security weapons while explicitly retaining the Qassam Brigades' full military arsenal.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Mar 25, 2026): 72,265 Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO as of Apr 21, 2026. Gaza reconstruction cost: $71.4B per UN-EU-World Bank joint assessment (Apr 21, 2026).
